slides.lead: Demo presentation of the template. All text is sample (dummy) content and serves only to showcase the styles.
slides.notes: Opening slide. Studio name, one-line subtitle and a meta row at the bottom. Highlight a word with em for the accent color.
Team slide. Uses colored initials instead of photos, so it needs no images.

slides.lead: Three parts, with room for questions.
slides.notes: Agenda as three large tiles. Good for opening a block.

slides.lead: Your story, your market and the biggest gaps.
slides.notes: Divider slide between blocks. Large heading on a colored gradient.

slides.lead: Logos, colors and copy are just the surface. The real value is the feeling people keep after they leave.
slides.footnote: // illustrative slide with an enlarged image
slides.notes: Hero slide: big heading, bold illustration, short caption. Great for a key point or a chapter open.

items: higher recall after the brand refresh
from kickoff to a launch-ready system
bullets: Measure brand impact, not just output volume.
Start where the story is weakest and the stakes are highest.
A sharp small campaign beats a vague big one.
slides.notes: KPI dashboard: three cards with an icon, big number and label, with supporting bullets below.

slides.lead: From a blank page to a system you can ship.
bullets: To your customers, your team and your market.
The positioning and the voice that set you apart.
Identity, color and type into one coherent system.
Guidelines and assets your team can actually use.
slides.notes: Two-column slide: bullets with a strong lead word on the left, illustration on the right. Text resizes itself.

slides.lead: Same brand, two paths and two levels of change.
cards: Polish the existing identity. Fast and low-risk.
A new system from the ground up. Bold and lasting.
Stretch the brand into new products and channels.
Keep the core, reinvent the campaign layer.
slides.notes: A 2x2 grid of cards. Good for comparisons or listing options.

slides.lead: Six typical building blocks of a brand system.
cards: The single idea your brand owns in the market.
Logo, color and type as one coherent set.
How the brand sounds across every channel.
Concepts that carry the brand into the world.
Clear rules so the brand stays consistent.
Assets ready for web, social and print.
slides.notes: A 3x2 grid with an icon in each card header. Great for an overview of services or capabilities.

items: The brand stabilizes, so we polish the fundamentals.
A campaign lands and reach grows fast.
Each launch builds equity for the next.
slides.notes: Numbered cards with small trend charts and an illustration on the right. Ideal for three possible futures.

slides.lead: Five steps, each with a clear deliverable.
slides.notes: A horizontal flow of steps with arrows. Each box has a heading and a small caption.

slides.quote: Design is not how it looks. It is how clearly it makes people feel what you mean.
slides.by: Sample quote to showcase the style
slides.notes: Quote slide with a bold left rule. Keep the attribution small.

slides.lead: Simple, but easy to forget under deadline.
bullets: A brand that says everything says nothing.
Every touchpoint sounds like the same studio.
Someone owns the brand and defends it.
slides.notes: A plain bullet slide with a strong lead word. Text scales up automatically to fill the space.

slides.lead: A bold launch fades fast without a system behind it.
slides.notes: Statement slide: one big sentence centered on the page. Use it for the key message.

slides.lead: What we do this week, this month and this quarter.
slides.notes: Second divider, opens the practical part.

slides.lead: We switch into the studio and build a sample mood board live.
slides.footnote: // placeholder slide for a demo or break
slides.notes: Placeholder slide. Centered heading, ideal for a live demo, a break or questions.
Closing slide in the style of the title. Contact and call to action.
